name: Multi-reward design + dynamic weighting (curriculum)
role: Define and dynamically weight the reward the coordination policy optimizes in the kinematic env: no-crash (dominant), low-idle/keep-moving, distance/shortest-path, smooth turns.
decision: Make this an explicit, tunable component. r = w1*(-collision) + w2*(progress_toward_goal) + w3*(-detour_vs_shortest_path) + w4*(turn_smoothness). 'Prioritize rewards dynamically' = curriculum: collision weight dominant first, anneal in efficiency rewards once crash-rate drops.
inputs: collision/near-miss signals from kinematic-env (geometric, calibrated to car footprint)
progress vs networkx Dijkstra shortest-path baseline (free from the osmnx graph)
school/POI proximity masks (from proximity-osm) to weight caution zones
outputs: reward-component telemetry for the demo dashboard

reward_components.name: low-idle / keep-moving
reward_components.note: reward PROGRESS toward goal, not raw speed (else cars circle to farm speed)

reward_components.name: turns / smoothness
reward_components.note: also keeps driving gentle -> smaller kinematic<->physics gap at demo time

reward_hacking_watchlist: idle forever to avoid all crashes -> idle penalty fixes it
drive in circles to farm 'moving' reward -> use progress-to-goal not raw speed
miscalibrated collision footprint -> phantom or missed crashes; calibrate first
risks: collision proxy must be calibrated to be a meaningful no-crash signal
